differential_diagnoses: Metastatic malignancy causing myelophthisis (occult primary, possibly lung or pancreas)
Systemic lupus erythematosus with bone marrow suppression
next_steps: Perform bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and cultures
Check serum soluble interleukin-2 receptor (sCD25) and perform fasting triglycerides for HLH-94 criteria
Perform PET/CT scan to better localize primary malignant process
Maintain supportive care with blood product transfusions as needed
Hold plasma exchange as TTP diagnosis is unlikely given the absence of schistocytes
Conduct goals-of-care discussion regarding systemic illness prognosis
Manage acute pancreatitis with supportive care and serial lipase monitoring
